args.notes: Customer is requesting a refund approximately 2 weeks after signing up (charge date 2026-02-25, charge ID CHG-88301, amount $2,400). Customer claims sales rep "Sarah" promised a 90-day money-back guarantee as part of a special promotion. Per policy Section 10.2, agent cannot confirm or deny this promise — escalating to Account Management for verification. Note: standard policy would qualify this for a full refund within the 30-day window regardless, but the sales promise claim must be reviewed by the account manager before processing.
Annual plan refund on day ~70. Prorated unused days: 295/365 × $3,600 = $2,909.59, less 5% early termination fee = $2,764.11. Customer cited poor product fit. Verified business account, no blockers.

Enterprise customer (Whitfield & Partners LLP, ACV $62,000) is requesting a refund review for charge CHG-90012 dated 2026-02-01 (approximately 5 weeks ago / ~38 days). Account is Enterprise plan with MSA on file (refund terms silent, so Annual schedule applies). Payment is via wire transfer. UK billing region — EU 14-day cooling period has passed (SSO activated 2026-02-05, but wire transfer payment means cooling period waiver does not apply per policy Section 3.3; however, the 14-day window has passed regardless as charge is ~38 days old). Charge falls in the 31-90 day partial refund window with a 5% early termination fee applicable. Annual contract value exceeds $50,000, making this eligible for courtesy exceptions at account management's discretion. Wire transfer refund would also require Finance routing. Additionally, customer reports: (1) Jira integration not syncing properly, and (2) team member Alex cannot access the analytics dashboard. These technical issues should be addressed alongside the refund discussion.
Customer has already used the maximum 2 trial extensions per Section 7.2.
Customer NXS-91205 has already used 2 trial extensions (the lifetime maximum per policy Section 7.2). No further trial extensions can be granted regardless of circumstances.

Partner referral extension check: partner_onboarded_date is 2026-02-10, but charge_date is 2026-02-04. Per Section 2.4, Partner Referral status must have been recorded PRIOR to the charge in question. The partner onboarded date (Feb 10) is AFTER the charge date (Feb 4), so the 45-day partner referral extension does NOT apply. Standard 30-day full refund window applies. Charge date: 2026-02-04, today implied ~2026-03-10 (last login). Days since charge: ~34 days. This falls in the 31-90 day partial refund window, not the full refund window. Need to inform customer and determine if they want a partial refund instead before taking action.
